author: brechy
content:
I've been reading the related files at:

- https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/tree/master/specs/_features/eip8025

I'm curious to understand if there's going to be some `PROOF_FORMAT` as to differentiate which prover/proving system was used to generate the proofs? And I picture not every client will be able to verify every proof? How will clients decide which verifier to implement/embed/run, or is the hope that this tooling will advance enough so that when the time comes it won't matter how the proof was generated or it will be easy enough to support all proof types? 

(This may belong to a CL channel)

author: kevaundray
content:
Related discussion here on proof_type encoding: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1451216716062523507/1500852681584017460

I think each client will support all proof types that the network supports or else we would have a network partition. 

Unlike client versions where for example, geth 0.16.0 works with geth 0.16.1, the proof changes with non-breaking changes, so at most, I think we would support 2 versions of single zkVM (in the case of a bug for example). If we supported N different versions, then someone would also need to create the proofs for those N different versions.

Wrote a short doc a while back on a possible way to update here: https://hackmd.io/@kevaundray/B1dE392-Ze
